TextENGNew: ...Even Satoko's snoring, listened to from this perch by the window, sounded like the gentle chirping of insects.
changeReason: Old omits the simile comparing snoring to 'gentle chirping of insects' (涼やかな虫の音) present in the Japanese.

TextENGNew: ``Zzzzzzzz......``
Satoko's loud snoring made it abundantly clear she was fast asleep.
``She snores so cutely.``
Hanyuu giggled and pretended to poke Satoko's nose.
changeReason: Old says Hanyuu 'tries to poke'; Japanese 突っつくような真似をする means 'pretended to poke,' which new correctly captures.

TextENGNew: I looked up at the sky.
...The moon was the same as always, and yet somehow its light seemed warmer than usual tonight.
``You seem to be in a good mood, Rika.``
I realized it only after Hanyuu pointed it out.
...I really was in a very good mood.
Otherwise, I'd never feel so leisurely inclined to enjoy the moon on such a stifling, windless night.
changeReason: Old translates 月 (moon) as 'sky' and 風のない (windless) as 'humid'; new correctly says 'moon' and 'stifling, windless.'

TextENGNew: As my spirits rose further, I felt the urge to toast this beautiful moonlit night.
I was reaching deep into the back of the closet, behind the winter futon, when Hanyuu spoke up in a suspicious tone.
changeReason: Old says Hanyuu sounded 'noticeably upset'; Japanese 怪訝そうな means suspicious/dubious tone, which new correctly captures.

TextENGNew: ``Rika... Don't you even think about drinking...``
``I deserve it.@k@vS12/05/180500237. Just tonight.``
Hanyuu can't stand the smell of wine. Apparently, even a tiny whiff makes her head spin.
changeReason: Old says wine smell makes Hanyuu feel 'giddy' (positive connotation); Japanese 目が回る means dizzy/head spinning (negative).

TextENGNew: Since whatever I consume, Hanyuu effectively consumes too, she always puts up a fuss whenever I try to drink.
Though really, she just assumes anything that smells like grapes must be wine -- she's completely mistaken.
...I wonder when she'll finally realize that what I've been drinking isn't wine at all, but plain grape juice.@k That's a small, secret pleasure I've discovered over these long, long years.
``No, you can't! You're too young to be drinking!``
``It's my body.@k@vS12/05/180500239. I'm going to make my own decisions.``
``No. I hate alcohol!@k@vS12/12/181100060. You can't drink!``
``I don't want to fight with you tonight.@k@vS12/05/180500241. I'll dilute it with orange juice so you can handle the taste.@k@vS12/05/180500242. Is that a deal?``
``That's not the point. I don't like the smell of alcohol.
So you can't drink!``
Hanyuu stomped and bounced around in protest.
Right next to Satoko's futon.
``Stop that! You're going to wake her up.``
``No I'm not.@k@vS12/12/181100064. Remember, you're the only one who can hear me.@k@vS12/12/181100065. Bleeeeh!``
``Hoh, just who do you think you are?``
I opened the refrigerator, took out a carton of orange juice and the container of punishingly spicy kimchi, and held them up for Hanyuu to see.
``...Au au au!!``

``Let me ask you a question.@k@vS12/05/180500246. Would you prefer if I ate this spicy kimchi to punish you,@k@vS12/05/180500247. or drink a sweet orange juice cocktail while enjoying this beautiful moon instead?``
Hanyuu clenched her lips while jumping up and down, ready to burst into tears.
Any normal child would be scolded and disciplined for being so noisy,@k but since no one besides me can hear Hanyuu, she's never been disciplined by anyone.
Then again, since Hanyuu is essentially like air, you couldn't give her a smack even if you wanted to.
As this reaction made abundantly clear, Hanyuu had an insatiable sweet tooth and was utterly incapable of handling anything spicy.
``Riiikaaa... Would you stop jumping around...?``
Satoko's voice came out of nowhere, and I hastily hid my glass.
changeReason: Old says 'Satoko woke up suddenly'; Japanese only says her voice was suddenly heard, not that she woke up.

TextENGNew: ...Naturally, the fact that I secretly bought grape juice and called it wine was a secret from Satoko.@k If she caught me, the lie I'd been telling Hanyuu would be exposed.
``What... time do you think it is...?@k@vS12/04/180400115. Zzzz.......``
She fell asleep again after speaking.
``See? She was bothered by you jumping around like that.@k@vS12/05/180500249. You nighttime nuisance.``
Just as Hanyuu's form was invisible to everyone but me, the sounds she made were generally inaudible to anyone but me.
But that was only 'generally' speaking.
Hanyuu unmistakably exists.
So there are rare people and rare moments when her presence and sounds can be perceived.
Of course, such people are few and far between, and even when it does happen, they usually dismiss it as their imagination.
Based on my experience...@k people in the terminal stages, like Satoko had once been, tend to perceive her more easily.
In Irie's terminology, that means the terminal L4 stage -- beyond all help.
'Terminal' really is an apt word for it.
changeReason: Old mistranslates entirely; Japanese says 'the word terminal is indeed apt,' while old says 'there's nothing that can be done anymore.'

TextENGNew: ...If you started seeing the form and hearing the voice of a being who shouldn't exist, anyone would question their own sanity and believe they'd reached the end.
changeReason: Old omits self-doubt about sanity (正気を疑い) and believing oneself terminal (末期だと信じる).

TextENGNew: Hanyuu stopped her bouncing protest and seemed to resign herself as I prepared for my moonlit evening.
changeReason: Old adds specific detail 'pouring alcohol into my glass' as an interpretation; JPN 月見を愉しむ準備 more broadly means 'preparing for moon-viewing.' New is more faithful.

TextENGNew: ...It's not as though I was drinking to torment Hanyuu.
If I could, I wanted her to be in good spirits and keep me company.
So I poured just a touch of my fake wine -- grape juice -- only enough for the aroma, then diluted it almost entirely with orange juice.
changeReason: Old says 'wine' omitting that it's actually grape juice (偽ワインーーブドウジュース); new preserves this key detail.

TextENGNew: And there it was: the world's most peculiar cocktail -- grape juice cut with orange juice.
changeReason: Old reduces to 'pretty much just juice'; Japanese describes 世にも奇妙な (the world's most peculiar) cocktail of grape juice with orange juice.

TextENGNew: ...Juice mixed with juice, packed with crushed ice.@k ...Somehow, it looked almost sophisticated.
``See, Hanyuu?@k@vS12/05/180500251. It's very diluted.``
I licked the edge of the glass to see how she'd react.
``I guess it's not that bad...``
``Good. I'll be drinking this tonight.@k@vS12/05/180500253. There's nothing to be afraid of anymore, all right?``
Apparently, the 'punishment' kimchi sitting on the counter this whole time had been terrifying Hanyuu.
When I put it back in the refrigerator, she finally wore a look of relief.
I took the glass and headed back to the window.
There was no breeze, but it seemed a little bit cooler.
``Hey, Hanyuu...@k@vS12/05/180500255. ...Do you sense it too? That something is different this time?``
``Are you talking about Keiichi and Rena...?``
``And probably Shion too.@k@vS12/05/180500257. ...Everyone is retaining fragments of memory from past worlds, however faint.@k@vS12/05/180500258. This has never happened before.``
changeReason: New replaces the third segment about using memories to avoid fatal mistakes with 'This has never happened before,' changing the meaning.

TextENGNew: ``Individually, I don't think it would be that unusual.@k@vS12/12/181100072. But for Keiichi, Rena, Shion -- for everyone to show these signs simultaneously... that is certainly rare.``
changeReason: Old says 'very strange' while Japanese とても珍しい means 'very rare/unusual'; new also adds specific character names from the Japanese.

TextENGNew: ``...I often liken my fate to a board game...@k@vS12/05/180500260. And this time, it's as though I rolled three dice and all of them came up six. That's how good my starting position feels.``

``I think that's a good way to describe it in the sense that we're starting closest to your desired future.@k@vS12/12/181100074. ......But.@k@vS12/12/181100075. I don't think this is the miracle that you've been waiting for...``
I knew what she was trying to say.@k ...Don't get your hopes up.
A die is designed from the start to include the possibility of landing on six.
changeReason: Old says die is 'designed to have 6 sides' (physical); Japanese means designed so landing on six is possible (probability).

TextENGNew: So rolling a six is neither a miracle nor anything extraordinary.
A true miracle would be rolling a single die and somehow getting a ten -- that's what Keiichi's feat in the previous world was.
changeReason: Old says 'my previous life'; Japanese 前回 refers to the previous world/iteration, significant in Higurashi's loop context.

TextENGNew: In other words, a miracle is something that would normally never happen.

Keiichi gave the doll to Mion,@k Rena spoke with her father,@k and Shion started taking care of Satoko after remembering her promise to Satoshi.@k That was all because they had those intentions somewhere in their hearts.
It was only a side of their die that was engraved there already.
But still, even if you couldn't call three dice rolling this well a miracle, it did mean good fortune.
``...I'm sure it was God's way of celebrating you finally feeling motivated to change your fate.``
``Hee hee...@k@vS12/05/180500262. I thought you were one of the gods.``
``You only treat me like a god when you're trying to tease me...``
``I was just kidding. Don't get upset.@k@vS12/05/180500264. Anyway, that's why I'm in such a good mood tonight.``
I lifted up the glass of orange wine and gave a toast to the moon.
It tasted like nothing but sweet orange juice...
It was funny to see Hanyuu making a happy face as I took a sip.
``.......I wonder if I can escape the death of 1983 this time.``
``If our goal is to escape from this board, then I don't think we'll ever have such a fortunate start again.``
No matter how hard I tried to prevent things from happening, there were some things I could never control.
It's such a huge letdown when that happens.
Even after trying so hard, something renders my efforts infertile.
...I'd probably been wasting my time for so long because I was trying to escape from that pain.
For the first time in my life, then, I've been blessed with a favorable situation.
Everything seems perfect in this world.
There's no reason for me to be depressed...
It's like the weather is perfect and the wind is blowing in just the right way.
If I failed to put my ship out to sea, there was nothing to pin the blame on.
I understood what Hanyuu was trying to say with the concerned look on her face.
If I didn't escape my death under this fortunate situation,@k I'd have to accept that my fate wasn't something that could be changed...
This is an ideal world where all of my friends overcame their problems.
I was always in a depressing world, but this time all the pieces of the puzzle matched.
If I couldn't change my fate in this world...
Then it was proof that I couldn't change it in any world.
That was the ending that I've been afraid of for a long time...
The loss of hope... and the death of my soul.
Of the pair of us, I was probably the only one who would die.
Hanyuu will most likely remain there even if my spirit disappears...
She must be afraid of being alone after I lose myself.@k That's why she's telling me not to expect too much.
I bit down on an ice chip inside my mouth. It was pleasantly cold.
``Hanyuu......@k@vS12/05/180500267. I know we've been over this many times, but I want you to think about me again...``
``Sure.@k@vS12/12/181100081. ...You want to know why you're being murdered in 1983?``
``Yes.@k@vS12/05/180500269. ............A strong will becomes fate.@k@vS12/05/180500270. That means that the reason I get murdered this year is because of someone's strong will.``
``I think you're right...``
``Who will benefit from killing Rika Furude...?``
``......If that person has a strong will, there must be a clear motive for them doing so.``
``It might sound odd coming from me...@k@vS12/05/180500273. but I think most people like Rika Furude.@k@vS12/05/180500274. I don't think any villager would have the motive to kill me.``
``The people who live in this village are all fond of you...``
``Thanks to you.``

``I'm just showing you my appreciation.@k@vS12/05/180500277. Anyway...@k@vS12/05/180500278. We now know that the Sonozaki family isn't the culprit...@k@vS12/05/180500279. I have no idea who else would want to kill me.``
``.........You know, this village is supposed to be the safest place in the world for you.``
``You're right.@k@vS12/05/180500281. It's supposed to be.@k@vS12/05/180500282. There are plenty of people who would protect me here.``
It's ironic.@k For a long time, I've been refusing help from all those people who were trying to keep me safe.
``........Rika.@k@vS12/12/181100088. I think we should rely on Irie...``
People in this village love and cherish me.
But no matter what, they never save me when I try to escape the fate of my death.
I spoke to several villagers in the past, but they never believed me and none of them saved me in the end.
They probably didn't believe me because I had no idea who was trying to kill me, or what their motive was.
If I could explain that, people might be more accepting of my story...
But there's nothing I can do about that.
No one would believe a little girl saying that she's going to be killed without rhyme or reason.
All they think of me as is the reincarnation of Oyashiro-sama and the leader of the Furude family.
They do cherish me, but they won't protect me with their lives.
On the other hand, Irie and his people are totally different.
They realize how special I am, and they would try to make sure that nothing happens to me.
If I confessed to them that my life was in danger, I'm sure they would do their best to prevent it.
They have all the means to protect me from a violent end.
However, none of it really worked in the past...
It's not that they're unreliable.
It's because Takano, the one responsible for protecting me, also gets killed on the same day every time.
In other words, asking her for help is completely useless.
She's very reliable, but she also isn't.
It's ironic that I'm the only one who knows the reason why they can never save me.
``I wonder if that's the murderer's plan...@k@vS12/05/180500285. Tomitake and Takano get killed because they're my guardians... Then I get killed after that...``
``I think Takano's death is definitely related to your own...``
``If it's the same fate... I mean, if the same suspect is killing them for the same motive...@k@vS12/05/180500287. I might be able to alter my own outcome by preventing their deaths.``
``I don't know if you remember, but we've tried that once before...``
The deaths of Takano and Tomitake mean the loss of my protection.
Because the two of them die, the suspect is able to kill me.
That's why I've tried to prevent their deaths somehow...
But they never believed me.
I tried so hard to persuade them, but they didn't listen.
I eventually gave up and just thought of their deaths as fated to happen.
It's the same hopeless feeling I used to have toward my parents when I managed to go farther back in time.
When I found out that death was inevitable, I lost interest in them...
After all that time had passed, I forgot how hard I used to try to prevent my parents' deaths...
``.......Besides, I thought you hated Irie and his people.``
``Yeah...@k@vS12/05/180500289. They look at us as nothing but their guinea pigs.``
I support them so I can help Satoko.
But it disgusts me to think that they're only using me as a test subject.
I've been resenting Irie for that reason.
Still, I should try to get closer to him.
After all, he's the only person who understands how important I am.
He's the main person I should be talking to, and he has the most potential for protecting me.
I can't avoid seeking help from him just because I don't like him.
I've asked him for help several times before...
But Tomitake and Takano get murdered even so, and it ruins everything.
After their deaths, Irie panics and becomes useless.
That's why I gave up on seeking help from him.
But in this world, I've been blessed with a positive situation.
I will never have as lucky a start as this again.
This is the best chance I'll ever get, so I should give it my best shot.
I really don't like him at all...
But he's not a bad person.
I should respect him more.
He's serious about helping Satoko, and he's not as much of a mad scientist as I'm inclined to think.
Takano is a strange person too, but she's also someone who can give me support.
But she thinks I'm only her guinea pig.
That's why I don't really want her help...
Tomitake is more reliable than Takano.
But the biggest problem is that he only visits this village a few times a year.@k ...On top of that, he always dies before I get killed, so he can never help me.
However, they really are the people I should be talking to.
If I can change the fate of their deaths... They should be able to save me from my own.
It's possible that the only reason I lived until 1983 was because of their effort...

``I'm going to approach them once more, and do what I can to try and save them.@k@vS12/05/180500291. ...Oh, don't worry.@k@vS12/05/180500292. I expect they'll be murdered anyways, so I'm not getting my hopes up.@k@vS12/05/180500293. So I'll be fine, even if this hope's betrayed.``
``Au... You're such a cruel girl...``
I chuckled at her comment.

``If they can escape their fated deaths, then I'm sure they'll greatly influence my own destiny as well.@k@vS12/05/180500295. ...And this time I'm going to try talking to them with the assumption they'll still be killed.

In short, I'll talk to them about how I'll be protected from my coming danger after they die.@k@vS12/05/180500297. If they issue such orders, then maybe the guard around me will be strengthened after they do.``
The protection they provide is probably the highest standard of security I could hope for.
Yet I lose that the moment Tomitake and Takano die.
So I never have their protection after Watanagashi.
And therefore, I get killed too.
In other words, that's it.
Whoever possesses the strong will to kill me actually just wants me dead.
Yet so long as those two are alive, there's tight security all around Hinamizawa.
That prevents the killer from getting to me.
So... to kill me, they must first kill Tomitake and Takano, the heart of that security.
Then my protection falls away, leaving me exposed...

``The more I reason it out, the more I realize their fates are connected to mine.@k@vS12/05/180500299. .........Thinking from that point of view, my destiny becomes inescapable the moment I abandon them to die.``
``You should know what you have to do first then...``
``Yes.@k@vS12/05/180500301. I have to think about saving their lives, before thinking about mine.``
It's kind of ironic.
I gave up on them because I was more determined to save my own life.
But I finally came to the conclusion that I have to help them first, before helping myself.
...But there's something I still don't understand.
Someone with convictions this strong is trying to kill me for some equally strong motive.
Yet no matter how often I debate it, I can't think of anyone who'd benefit from my death.
Not only that, but there should only be people who'd want to protect me...
Especially since they would do anything to protect me and the present environment.
They even eliminated my mother.
...To be perfectly honest, I don't even care about her anymore.
I never got along with her, and she's the one who took my father's death the wrong way, making a huge deal out of it like she did.
She got what she deserved.
I'm a valuable guinea pig for them, and I can be a 'bomb' too.
No one benefits from making me explode.
There's only disaster awaiting them if that happens.
Lots of people will be lost, and nobody would gain anything from that.
According to Takano, my death would be like the eruption of a volcano in the middle of the village.
I'm not interested in what happens after my death, but they're really concerned about it.
They're watching me every day to ensure that I don't accidentally hit my head on the corner of a block of tofu and die.
It's fair to say that they are here to prevent the 'eruption' from happening.
I'd be suspicious of them if they were going to benefit in some way from my death.
But as everyone knows, that can't possibly be the case.
My death would create nothing but a disaster.
They're benefiting from my being alive right now.
``...Perhaps it's not about gain, but about a grudge...
Maybe someone has a strong grudge against my family, and kills me not knowing that I'm like a bomb to this village.``
That's why I was suspicious of the Three Families, especially the Sonozaki family.
However... after observing them in numerous worlds, I feel that's not the case.
I felt that way in several lives, but it always had something to do with that incident with Keiichi and the doll.
It's clear that there's nothing going on between the Sonozaki family and mine.
``In that case... it must be some religious fanatic...``
If it's not about profit or a grudge, it might be someone who's secretly a deranged cultist...
We've already had a series of murders four years in a row under the name of Oyashiro-sama's curse.
Timing-wise, it would somewhat interesting if I, the reincarnation of Oyashiro-sama, were killed on the fifth year.
...I couldn't stand to be murdered for such a whimsical reason, but such a motive actually seemed fitting.
Ironically, I would have to suspect Takano, if that's the case.
She's well known for being an eccentric researcher of local histories.
She's only interested in the village's history because she already knows who I am.
She's doing the research based on the notion that I'm important.
So she should have no reason to kill me.
She would desire to keep me close to her, as a research sample.
It's hard to believe that she would kill her favorite toy.
She might be willing to kill someone who would try to harm her little toy, though.

``...I will never find out no matter how hard I think.@k@vS12/05/180500305. I just know that someone is definitely going to kill me.@k@vS12/05/180500306. I don't care about anything else, as long as I can escape that fate.``
No, I take that back.
I want to escape my deadly fate,@k but I also want all of my friends to be happy@k and I want the rest of my life to be happy too.
I don't want to hide myself in the forest forever just to get away from my murderer.
...In fact I've tried that once before, but I still met with the same ending.
Living in the forest was a horrible experience.
I think I managed to survive until July that time.@k ...That might be the longest I ever lived.
I smiled bitterly and finished off the rest of the orange wine in the glass.
I'd have to talk to Irie again.
I don't like him, but his people are my only hope.
``It's getting late...@k@vS12/05/180500308. I should go to sleep now.``
``I think so too.@k@vS12/12/181100096. Good night.``
I lay down and closed my eyes, but I couldn't fall asleep right away.
Suddenly, I remembered Rena's words out of nowhere.
``It was no good when I was thinking about it on my own.``
I wasn't thinking about it on my own...@k I was talking it out with Hanyuu...
``Now I know.@k It's important to talk to other people.@k Friends have greater meaning than I thought.@k You can talk to them about anything. I finally realized that...``
After I ask Takano for help, should I also talk to Keiichi...?
I've told him before in my previous lives.
But he never believed me then...
